source_jobs.title: Agentic Product Design Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT APPLY
 
APPLY is the Agentic Customer Experience (ACx) partner for the world's most ambitious consumer and entertainment brands. We bring together deep domain expertise across Retail, CPG, Sports, and Media with AI-native delivery capability, designing and delivering agentic solutions that turn CX vision into commercial reality. We are the partner of choice for brands like Arc'teryx, NFL, Lululemon, and Kraft Heinz. For more information, visit applydigital.com.
 
LOCATION: APPLY is hybrid/remote friendly. The preferred candidate should be based in Canada, working in hours that align to ET (Eastern Timezone) or PT (Pacific Timezone).
 
THE ROLE:


As an Agentic Product Design Lead, you will be a hands-on senior design leader at the intersection of craft, systems thinking, and AI-native workflows. Reporting to the Head of Design, you will lead complex client engagements end-to-end — owning design quality across multi-product and multi-platform projects while actively designing alongside AI agents as a core part of how you work.
This is a role for a practitioner who leads by doing. You bring deep expertise in design systems — from token architecture and component governance to CMS integration and developer handoff — and you know how to operationalize that expertise within modern agentic workflows using tools like Claude for Design, Figma MCP, and BMAD. You are equally comfortable presenting design rationale to senior client stakeholders and rolling up your sleeves to build the systems and screens yourself.
You will mentor and guide designers at various levels, champion design system standards across your engagements, and contribute to evolving how Apply Digital designs and delivers in an AI-accelerated world.

source_jobs.title: MarTech Solutions Architect
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT APPLY
 
APPLY is the Agentic Customer Experience (ACx) partner for the world's most ambitious consumer and entertainment brands. We bring together deep domain expertise across Retail, CPG, Sports, and Media with AI-native delivery capability, designing and delivering agentic solutions that turn CX vision into commercial reality. We are the partner of choice for brands like Arc'teryx, NFL, Lululemon, and Kraft Heinz. For more information, visit applydigital.com.


About Us: Apply Digital’s Marketing Services division is a rapidly expanding full-service Martech team with team members in the US, Europe, LATAM, and Asia. As a personalization agency, we bridge the gap between creative, marketing, and strategy. We specialize in cross-channel content strategy, creation, and distribution (email, paid social, push, SMS), and MarTech implementations. We work with 50+ clients, ranging in size from companies like YETI, Upside, and Nutrafol to established marquee brands like A+E Networks, Afterpay, and Stanley Black & Decker.
 
The Role: We are looking to bring on a full-time Solutions Architect with a particular strength in integrating Customer Data Platforms and marketing automation tools. The ideal candidate is someone who is wholly invested in a full-time career in MarTech, AdTech, and product, and wants to grow with our company.
 
The candidate has the opportunity to provide expert consultation across a wide variety of clients, directly influencing the technical outcome of their tech stack.
 
LOCATION: Apply Digital is hybrid/remote friendly. The preferred candidate should be based in Canada, working in hours that align to ET (Eastern Timezone) or PT or in Spain, working in hours that overlap with ET (Eastern Timezone) or PT (Pacific Timezone).

source_jobs.title: People & Culture Director
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT APPLY
 
APPLY is the Agentic Customer Experience (ACx) partner for the world's most ambitious consumer and entertainment brands. We bring together deep domain expertise across Retail, CPG, Sports, and Media with AI-native delivery capability, designing and delivering agentic solutions that turn CX vision into commercial reality. We are the partner of choice for brands like Arc'teryx, NFL, Lululemon, and Kraft Heinz. For more information, visit applydigital.com.
 
LOCATION: APPLY is hybrid/remote friendly. The preferred candidate should be based in the Greater Toronto Area of Canada, working in hours that align to ET.
 
THE ROLE:
 


You'll lead a globally distributed team of HRBPs and People Operations specialists, ensuring that every employee has an exceptional experience — from the moment they join to the moments that matter most in their careers. Working closely with the Chief People Officer, you will translate People strategy into consistent, high-quality execution: embedding AI-powered tools and workflows across the function, championing our culture, and ensuring our HR operations run with precision and compliance across multiple jurisdictions.
This is a hands-on leadership role for a builder who is equally comfortable coaching an HRBP through a complex employee relations case and redesigning a clunky workflow. You bring strong EQ, a bias for automation, and a genuine belief that a lean, AI-augmented team can outperform a large transactional one.

source_jobs.title: Principal Agentic Engineer (Back-end)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT APPLY
 
APPLY is the Agentic Customer Experience (ACx) partner for the world's most ambitious consumer and entertainment brands. We bring together deep domain expertise across Retail, CPG, Sports, and Media with AI-native delivery capability, designing and delivering agentic solutions that turn CX vision into commercial reality. We are the partner of choice for brands like Arc'teryx, NFL, Lululemon, and Kraft Heinz. For more information, visit applydigital.com.
 
LOCATION: APPLY is hybrid/remote friendly. The preferred candidate should be based in Canada, working in hours that align to ET (Eastern Timezone) or PT (Pacific Timezone).
 
THE ROLE:


The Principal Agentic Engineer (Back-end) is a senior technical leader within our engineering organization, responsible for designing, building, and scaling robust backend systems that support modern, AI-powered digital products. This role combines deep backend engineering expertise with strong architectural judgment, AI-system fluency, and client-facing leadership.
 
As a Principal Agentic Engineer, you will lead technical direction across complex backend systems, mentor engineers, and partner closely with clients and internal teams to deliver production-grade solutions. You will play a critical role in designing distributed, cloud-native architectures and integrating large language models (LLMs), vector databases, and AI agents into real-world applications. You will engineer teams of coding agents to accomplish implementation requirements for the systems you will be building.
 
You bring proven leadership at a Staff or Lead level, with a track record of delivering complex software initiatives end-to-end. You operate with high autonomy and can take loosely defined goals and turn them into well-architected, shipped software.

You will lead frontend technical direction, mentor engineers, and collaborate closely with backend, product, and design teams to deliver production-ready solutions. The role requires deep expertise in modern frontend frameworks, AI-assisted development workflows, and cloud-connected architectures. You will engineer teams of coding agents to accomplish implementation requirements for the systems you will be building.
 
You bring a strong leadership track record at the Staff or Lead level, operating with high autonomy and turning ambiguous product or business goals into shipped, production-ready software.

source_jobs.description:
ABOUT APPLY
 
APPLY is the Agentic Customer Experience (ACx) partner for the world's most ambitious consumer and entertainment brands. We bring together deep domain expertise across Retail, CPG, Sports, and Media with AI-native delivery capability, designing and delivering agentic solutions that turn CX vision into commercial reality. We are the partner of choice for brands like Arc'teryx, NFL, Lululemon, and Kraft Heinz. For more information, visit applydigital.com.
 
LOCATION: APPLY is hybrid friendly with remote options available if needed. The preferred candidate should be based in (or within a location commutable to) the Greater London Area of the United Kingdom, working in hours that align to the UK timezone.
 
THE ROLE:


We are seeking an experienced Programme Manager to lead the planning, execution, and operational governance of complex, multi-stream delivery programs. This role is responsible for ensuring predictable, scalable, and high-quality delivery across portfolios of projects by implementing strong delivery frameworks, operational controls, and execution discipline.
You will work across cross-functional teams (product, engineering, design, data, and change) to drive execution excellence, optimize delivery processes, and manage risks, dependencies, and resource constraints across programs. You will partner closely with senior stakeholders, PMO, and delivery leadership to ensure programs are aligned with strategic priorities and operational standards.
This is a hands-on execution and delivery operations role;ideal for someone who thrives in structured environments, complex delivery ecosystems, and continuous improvement.

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT APPLY
 
APPLY is the Agentic Customer Experience (ACx) partner for the world's most ambitious consumer and entertainment brands. We bring together deep domain expertise across Retail, CPG, Sports, and Media with AI-native delivery capability, designing and delivering agentic solutions that turn CX vision into commercial reality. We are the partner of choice for brands like Arc'teryx, NFL, Lululemon, and Kraft Heinz. For more information, visit applydigital.com.
 
LOCATION: APPLY is hybrid/remote-friendly. The preferred candidate should be based in the Greater Los Angeles area, with working hours that align to PST. This is a hybrid role and requires regular on-site presence at our client's office, at least 3 days a week. 
 
THE ROLE:


As a Senior Product Consultant embedded with our client in sports entertainment, you will be responsible for driving the delivery of world-class video and advertising experiences across web, mobile, and connected devices. This is a technically demanding space — spanning live streaming infrastructure, video delivery pipelines, and programmatic advertising — and you will need to be comfortable going deep with engineering teams, evaluating technical tradeoffs, and holding your own in conversations with vendors and architects. Working alongside the Senior Product Manager on the Video team and Director of Product on the Ads team, you will take ownership of roadmap execution and day-to-day delivery across these two high-impact product areas — Video and Ads — collaborating with engineering, design, and business stakeholders to ensure we ship with quality and velocity.
 
You will be an active contributor to product strategy: leading roadmap conversations, identifying new opportunities, developing use cases, and helping define initiatives from the ground up. You'll own meaningful scope across the product lifecycle — from discovery and problem definition through to launch — with direct exposure to and influence with senior product and business leadership at one of the world's most recognized sports entertainment brands.

THE ROLE:


As a Strategy Lead, you'll help our clients grow through Agentic Customer Experience (ACx): the practice of designing brand experiences that show up with intelligence, relevance and continuity across every part of a customer's ecosystem.

Reporting into the Strategy team, you'll lead ACx initiatives working with cross-functional teams to bring strategies to life, grow client relationships into new opportunities, and help evolve our strategic practice for an agent-mediated future.
 
Read our full ACx report here: https://www.applydigital.com/insights/learn/the-acx-report/

source_jobs.title: Tech Lead (Salesforce CRM)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT APPLY
 
APPLY is the Agentic Customer Experience (ACx) partner for the world's most ambitious consumer and entertainment brands. We bring together deep domain expertise across Retail, CPG, Sports, and Media with AI-native delivery capability, designing and delivering agentic solutions that turn CX vision into commercial reality. We are the partner of choice for brands like Arc'teryx, NFL, Lululemon, and Kraft Heinz. For more information, visit applydigital.com.
 
LOCATION: APPLY is hybrid/remote-friendly. The preferred candidate should be based in Latin America, preferably working in hours that align to PT (Pacific Timezone) or ET (Eastern Timezone). Candidates located in Santiago, Chile are able to work out of our Santiago office as remote/hybrid employees. Candidates located outside of Santiago, Chile will be fully remote employees.
 
THE ROLE:


The Tech Lead is the single technical owner accountable for the successful delivery of a Salesforce-centric digital onboarding platform built for a global credit-bureau client. Salesforce is the heart of the solution — it orchestrates the entire B2B registration and onboarding journey: lead capture, identity-validation outcomes, disposition logic, and contract e-signature. You own the Salesforce build end to end and keep it aligned with the surrounding integration and infrastructure layers.
This is a high-autonomy role and the sole senior Salesforce voice on the engagement. You take architectural direction and turn it into shipped, production-grade Salesforce metadata — objects, Record Types, Flows, Apex, LWC, permission model, and managed-package configuration (DocuSign eSignature). You also hold the technical relationship with the integration microservices and cloud platform that read from and write to the org: you don't build those daily, but you define the contracts, guard the data model, and make sure a schema or automation change never blindsides them.
Here is the truth: this is a Salesforce Platform (Core/CRM) role. We're looking for a builder who lives in the Salesforce org — declarative and programmatic — and who is senior enough to own the platform-level decisions that everything else depends on.
